Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/montevideo-v1 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.

content.text: Now let me check the GitHub repo info to create the issue.

content.content: marcus-sa/brain

input.command:
gh issue create --title "Add /chat/:id route for deep-linking conversations" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Problem

All chat happens on a single `/chat` route with conversation state managed entirely in React component state. This means:

- **Refreshing the page loses the active conversation** — user is dropped back to no conversation selected
- **No deep linking** — can't share or bookmark a specific conversation
- **Browser back/forward doesn't navigate between conversations**
- **Opening a conversation from external context (e.g. feed "Discuss" action) has no stable URL**

## Proposed Solution

Add a `/chat/:id` route so each conversation gets its own URL.

### Client-side changes

- **`router.tsx`**: Add `/chat/$conversationId` route alongside the existing `/chat` route
- **`chat-page.tsx`**: Read `conversationId` from route params; load that conversation on mount
- **`use-chat-session.ts`**: When selecting a conversation, navigate to `/chat/:id` instead of just updating internal state. When creating a new conversation, redirect to `/chat/:newId` after the first message returns a `conversationId`
- **Sidebar**: Conversation links should point to `/chat/:id`

### Behavior

| URL | Behavior |
|-----|----------|
| `/chat` | New/empty conversation (current behavior) |
| `/chat/:id` | Load and display specific conversation |

### Edge cases

- Invalid or inaccessible conversation ID → redirect to `/chat` with error toast
- Conversation belongs to a different workspace → 404 or redirect

## Current Architecture Reference

- Router: `app/src/client/router.tsx` (TanStack Router)
- Chat page: `app/src/client/routes/chat-page.tsx`
- Session hook: `app/src/client/hooks/use-chat-session.ts`
- Conversation API: `GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/conversations/:conversationId`
- Sidebar API: `GET /api/workspaces/:workspaceId/sidebar`
EOF
)"
input.description: Create GitHub issue for chat/:id route

content.content: https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/issues/86

content.text:
Created: **https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/issues/86**

The issue covers adding `/chat/:id` routing so conversations get deep-linkable URLs. Key changes would be in the TanStack Router config, chat page, and the session hook — the server-side conversation loading API already exists.

message.content: create gh issue for project route /project/:id

content.text: Let me explore the current project-related routing and data structures.

input.prompt:
Explore the project-related routing and UI in this codebase. I need to understand:
1. What project-related routes currently exist (client-side and server-side)
2. How projects are displayed currently (sidebar, home page, any project detail views)
3. What project data is available from the API (project entities, their fields, relationships)
4. Whether there's any existing `/project/:id` route or project detail page
5. What project-related API endpoints exist

Look at files like:
- Client-side router configuration (router.tsx)
- Home page and how it displays projects
- Sidebar and project listings
- Server-side routes for projects/entities
- Shared contracts/types for project data
- Any entity detail endpoints

Report back with a clear summary of the current state.
